title: Maritime Profile Key Figures (fleet, port calls, seafarers) — Comparator Countries
notes: 10 countries (USA, Venezuela, Spain, Portugal, Greece, Türkiye, Argentina, Saudi Arabia, Russia, South Korea), one 2024 snapshot each: national-flag fleet DWT & ship count, fleet under national ownership regardless of flag, container port throughput, ship building/recycling (GT), number of seafarers, number of port calls, plus GDP/population/trade context fields UNCTAD prints alongside. Iran's own maritime profile was extracted separately and more deeply in unctad_maritime_series/ (not this file) — cross-reference that for the Iran side of any comparison. No existing chart_id in CHART_REGISTRY.csv covers maritime/shipping at all (WDI's IS.SHP.GOOD.TU etc. cover different angles); this is a wholly new comparator category.

title: Argentina — Mineral Production, Major Commodities (USGS Minerals Yearbook)
notes: Metals, industrial minerals, and mineral fuels production series for Argentina, matching the commodity mix already used for Iran's own USGS-derived chart_ids (e.g. iran_chromite_production_1961_1980, iran_copper_production series) so the two are directly comparable commodity-by-commodity. Registered as ONE chart_id per country here (not one per commodity, unlike Iran's finer-grained registration) given the breadth-over-depth scope of this comparator batch — dozens of commodities per country in the underlying CSV; splitting into per-commodity chart_ids matching Iran's own granularity is a reasonable future pass.

title: Portugal — Population, Employment & Unemployment Rate (INE/BdP long series)
notes: Headline population/employment indicators (Q1.1), resident population by age group and demographic rates (Q1.2), and unemployment rate by sex/age group (Q1.11, 1983-2020).

title: Portugal — Gross Fixed Capital Formation by Asset Type (INE/BdP long series)
notes: Investment (FBCF) at current prices by fixed-asset type.

title: Portugal — GDP & National Expenditure Headline Series (INE/BdP long series)
notes: Q3.1: headline production/expenditure/income indicators (GDP at current prices, nominal GDP growth, GDP per capita, labor productivity). Q3.6.1: National Expenditure at current prices (domestic demand, final consumption, GFCF, exports/imports).

title: Portugal — Consumer Price Index, Level & Annual % Change (INE/BdP long series)
notes: Annual CPI index (2012=100) by COICOP class/group, and annual % change.

title: Portugal — Monetary Aggregates (INE/BdP long series)
notes: Q5.1: Monetary Synthesis 1947-1998 (pre-euro escudo-era aggregates). Q5.2: Euro-era monetary aggregates (Portugal's national contribution to euro-area M2/M3), 1999-2020.

title: Portugal — Government Revenue, Expenditure & Public Debt (INE/BdP long series)
notes: Q6.1: general government revenue and expenditure. Q6.3: gross consolidated general-government debt at nominal value (Maastricht debt definition).

title: Portugal — Balance of Payments Summary (INE/BdP long series)
notes: Current & capital account, financial account, summary balance-of-payments lines.

title: Portugal — Banco de Portugal Central Bank Balance Sheet (Assets & Liabilities)
notes: Genuine central-bank balance sheet (net foreign assets, gold, credit to government, monetary base, banknotes/coin, government deposits, own resources) in million escudos — a different angle than the monetary-aggregates series above (assets/liabilities, not just aggregates). Source table continues to 1995 per its own index but this extraction stopped at 1976 (see portugal_historical_series/README.md for why); a future pass could extend it.

title: Spain — Real & Nominal GDP, GDP per Capita, 1850-2000 (Carreras & Tafunell)
notes: GDP at factor cost and market prices, both constant-1995-peseta and current-peseta, plus GDP per capita — a genuinely long-run (150-year) academic reconstruction, the Spanish-history equivalent of Maddison-style deep GDP series. Only 3 of ~15 Cuadros in this compendium's income-and-wealth chapter were extracted; see README for the many more available.

title: Spain — Price Indices & CPI, 1800-2000 (Carreras & Tafunell)
notes: Cuadro 16.19: five independent historians' alternative price-index estimates, 1800-1958 (1913=100), sparse. Cuadro 16.20: official-style Consumer Price Index (urban/non-urban/general), 1939-2000 (base 1983=100). Together span two centuries of Spanish prices.

title: Saudi Arabia — National Accounts Aggregates: GDP, GNI, National Income, Saving (GASTAT)
notes: GASTAT's own headline summary table: GDP -> Net Factor Income -> GNI -> Consumption of Fixed Capital -> Net National Income -> Net Current Transfers -> Disposable Income -> Final Consumption -> Gross/Net Saving, all SAR million, 2024 marked preliminary.

title: Saudi Arabia — GDP by Economic Activity, Investment & National Income Detail (GASTAT)
category: Comparator: Industry / Value Added by Sector
notes: Table 1: GDP by ISIC economic-activity section/division at current prices (749 rows — granular sector detail down to 2-digit ISIC). Table 5: Gross Fixed Capital Formation by asset type. Table 6: GNI factor-income decomposition. Table 12: misc indicators (tourism GDP contribution, household saving rate, SME share of GDP, trade/GDP ratio), 2024 only.

title: Saudi Arabia — Consumer Price Index, Annual & Monthly (GASTAT)
notes: Annual-average CPI index level and % change by COICOP-style section (2025 vs 2024), plus monthly year-over-year general-index inflation (May 2025 - May 2026) and by-division year-over-year change for May 2026. Chart-derived values (Figures 1 & 2 of the May-2026 bulletin) were verified against a 300dpi PNG crop of the source chart, not read from text order alone.

title: Venezuela — Macroeconomic Aggregates: GDP, Inflation, Fiscal & External Sector (OVF/IIES-UCAB, independent)
notes: INDEPENDENT source (Instituto de Investigaciones Económicas y Sociales, Universidad Católica Andrés Bello — an academic institution, not the government), used deliberately in place of Venezuela's own statistics agency (BCV) given BCV's well-documented recent-years reliability problems (suspended CPI publication during the 2014-2019 hyperinflation crisis, widely-noted gaps since) — see venezuela_ovf_series/README.md for the full rationale, matching this project's documented policy of preferring a credible independent source over a compromised official one. Single continuous table covering real GDP growth, M2/monetary-base growth, CPI inflation (peaking at 130,060.2% in 2018), restricted-public-sector revenue/expenditure/fiscal balance (% GDP), external public debt (% exports), exports/imports/reserves (US$bn), real official exchange-rate index, and average Merey 16 crude oil price.

title: Venezuela in Global/Regional Context — IMF GDP Growth & Inflation Projections, 2021-2024 (via OVF/IIES-UCAB)
notes: World/developed/developing-economy aggregates plus 12 individual comparator countries (including Argentina and Russia, already in this project's core country set) alongside Venezuela — real GDP growth (Cuadro 1) and CPI inflation (Cuadro 2), both IMF-sourced, compiled by OVF/IIES-UCAB rather than pulled directly from IMF WEO (this project's existing imf-weo indicators could supersede this for the non-Venezuela rows if ever cross-checked; kept here as-is since it's the Venezuela row specifically that has no other source in this project).
